title: Case Study: Harnessing Mobility Data for Crisis Response, Nyiragongo

indicators_measured.name: Number of people affected by disasters
Internally displaced persons (share due to disaster)
Number of missing persons due to disaster

indicators_targeted.name: People affected by disasters
indicators_targeted.rationale: 400,000+ people displaced when Mount Nyiragongo lava destroyed 3,500+ homes, schools, and health centers near Goma

indicators_targeted.name: Disaster-related internal displacement
indicators_targeted.rationale: Tracking displacement to Bukavu, Rutshuru, and Sake-Masisi for targeted aid and recovery planning

indicators_targeted.name: Under-5 mortality risk from disease outbreaks
indicators_targeted.rationale: Overcrowded shelters increased cholera risk; mobility data enabled targeted health interventions

summary: In May 2021, Mount Nyiragongo erupted near Goma, DRC, destroying 3,500+ homes, schools, and health centers and displacing 400,000+ people in a region already facing chronic conflict. Traditional on-the-ground population assessments were too slow and costly for rapid response. Flowminder partnered with Vodacom RDC to analyze anonymized mobile Call Detail Records (CDRs), mapping displacement patterns to Bukavu, Rutshuru, and Sake-Masisi. This enabled targeted delivery of shelter, food, water, sanitation, and hygiene supplies; identification of high-density areas for cholera containment; and tracking of gradual return to Goma for recovery planning. Data became publicly available ~2 months post-eruption, limiting immediate impact but demonstrating scalability. Methods include anonymized CDR mobility analysis, population displacement mapping, privacy-preserving protocols, and complementarity with satellite imagery and community assessments.
